preview_text:
The model favors Uzbekistan at 47%, assigning DR Congo a 23% win probability and rating a draw at 30%. The statistical forecast thus sees the underdog with roughly double the chance of victory compared to the favorite on paper.

The spread across outcomes reflects genuine uncertainty. The model's 24-percentage-point gap between the favorite and underdog is material but not decisive; a draw sits between them at 30%, suggesting the match is unlikely to be settled by a comfortable margin. Competitive balance tilts toward Uzbekistan, yet the substantial draw probability indicates high variance and multiple plausible scorelines.

The market consensus (across sportsbooks and Polymarket) diverges sharply from the model. Books price DR Congo at 41% to win versus the model's 23%—a gap of 18 percentage points—and price Uzbekistan at 30% rather than 47%. Draw assessments are broadly aligned near 28–29%. The dashboard does not adjudicate which source is more accurate; the divergence is large enough that bettors and forecasters should weigh both signals before forming a view.
